# Mike Herak

> Personal essay site. Essays on whatever I can't stop thinking about - reading,
> thinking tools, consciousness, AI, and wherever else it leads. Some essays follow
> "trails" - passages traced across many books around one question (syntopic reading).

Quote and cite freely with a link back.

## Essays

- [Thoughts index](https://www.mikeherak.com/): all essays, newest first
- [RSS feed](https://www.mikeherak.com/feed.xml): full-content feed
- [The Synthesis Nobody Writes](https://www.mikeherak.com/thoughts/the-synthesis-nobody-writes): Mortimer Adler called reading across many books at once - around a question you bring to them - the highest level of reading. Past a handful of books it stops being thinking and turns into bookkeeping. So I built a tool for the bookkeeping part.
- [The Difference Between Productive Obsession and Presence](https://www.mikeherak.com/thoughts/the-difference-between-productive-obsession-and-presence): More of us are replacing idle scrolling with building something of our own - a business, a craft, a second act. That's real progress. But compulsive creating and compulsive consuming can share the same root - an inability to be still.
- [Two Maps, One Conversation](https://www.mikeherak.com/thoughts/two-maps-one-conversation): I built an emotional-intelligence framework with my AI - each of us creating our own map of what we care about - and it pulled me into the deepest thinking on consciousness I've done.

## Author

- Mike Herak - [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mike-herak/)
